channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: British tennis player Arthur Fery advances to Wimbledon fourth round after long match against Zizou Bergs
item.description: Britain’s Arthur Fery delivered an extraordinary performance at Wimbledon, overcoming a two-set deficit against Belgium’s Zizou Bergs in a five-set thriller that lasted four hours and 38 minutes. The ...

item.title: Ashwanth Valthapa's Cricket Journey and TNPL Auction Success
item.description: Ashwanth Valthapa, a 25-year-old mystery spinner from Tamil Nadu, has experienced a remarkable transformation in his cricketing career. Initially a wicketkeeper-batter, he shifted to spin bowling afte...

item.title: East Africa promotes Kiswahili through AI and innovation initiatives
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) is urging citizens to actively participate in advancing a new strategic direction led by the East African Kiswahili Commission (KAKAMA). This initiative focuses on lev...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarter-Finals with 3-0 Win Over Canada
item.description: Morocco secured a 3-0 victory against Canada in the FIFA World Cup 2026 last-16 match, advancing to the quarter-finals for the second consecutive time. Azzedine Ounahi scored twice, with the first goa...

item.title: Jeffrey Santos Rejects 477K Offer in 'Deal or No Deal' Contest
item.description: The news article discusses a segment from the Philippine television show 'Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal,' where contestant Jeffrey Santos faced a pivotal decision. During the episode, Santos was presented...

item.title: Parental frustration over school resourcing leads to rising workplace violence in ACT schools
item.description: Australian Education Union ACT branch secretary Patrick Judge reported a significant rise in occupational violence incidents involving parents and community members at ACT public schools. Data from 20...

item.title: Springboks Dominate England in Nations Championship Opener
item.description: The Springboks delivered a commanding performance against England in their Nations Championship opener, securing a 45-21 victory at Ellis Park. Despite losing key players Siya Kolisi and Eben Etzebeth...

item.title: Sam Larry Involved in Lagos Road Accident; Bouncer Killed
item.description: Nigerian music promoter and socialite Sam Larry was hospitalised following a severe road accident on Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road on July 4, 2026. The collision involved Larry's vehicle and a trailer, r...

item.title: France to Face Paraguay in FIFA World Cup Round of 16
item.description: The FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash between France and Paraguay took place at the Philadelphia Stadium on July 4, 2026. France's coach Didier Deschamps made a tactical adjustment by replacing injured...

item.title: Serena Williams Withdraws From Wimbledon Doubles Match Due to Knee Injury
item.description: Serena Williams has withdrawn from her doubles match against her sister Venus at Wimbledon due to a right knee injury, as revealed in an Instagram post. The 44-year-old athlete sustained the injury du...

item.title: Second Season of 'The Ramparts of Ice' Anime Announced for October 2026

item.title: France faces Paraguay in World Cup round of 16 as Morocco end Canada's run
item.description: In the FIFA World Cup 2026 round of 16, France faced Paraguay in a high-stakes match, with France dominating possession (90% ) and completing 77/85 passes compared to Paraguay's 4/9. Coach Didier Desc...

item.title: Bam Margera Rules Out Jackass Reunion, Expresses No Desire to Reconnect with Former Cast Members
item.description: Bam Margera, a former member of the stunt group Jackass, has firmly ruled out any possibility of a reunion with his former castmates, stating he never wants to see them again. This comes after he was ...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarter-Finals for Second Straight Tournament
item.description: Morocco secured their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals by defeating co-hosts Canada 3-0 in a Round of 16 clash at Houston Stadium. The African champions, led by Azzedine Ounahi and Souf...

item.title: Milly Alcock Reflects on Fame, Pressure, and Finding Ground as Supergirl
item.description: The article explores Milly Alcock's journey as she navigates the pressures of fame while portraying the superhero role in *Supergirl*. Alcock discusses her personal struggles with self-doubt, toxic fa...

item.title: SUV Crashes into Creek in Quezon City, Driver Injured
item.description: A SUV crashed into a creek in Quezon City, resulting in the driver's injury. According to reports, the incident occurred when the vehicle lost control and plunged into the water. Authorities are inves...

item.title: Tanzanian fans face health risks from late-night World Cup viewing habits
item.description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup progresses to its knockout stages, Tanzanian football fans are increasingly sacrificing sleep to watch matches broadcast from North America. The time difference of eight hou...

item.title: Philippines Allocates P550 Million for Seed Storage Facilities to Ensure Crop Supply
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) has allocated P550 million to construct seed storage facilities across the Philippines to mitigate weather-related disruptions in crop production. Agriculture Secret...

item.title: South Africa's Rugby Team Faces Uncertainty as Key Players Miss Scotland Test
item.description: South Africa's rugby team faces significant uncertainty ahead of their Nations Championship clash against Scotland, with key players potentially missing the match. Coach Rassie Erasmus confirmed that ...
